content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 20, 1993

Ben Adams
2019 No. Beverly Drive
Beverly Hills, CA 90210

Re: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. G-93-306

Dear Mr. Adams:

We have received your request for advice regarding the
Lanterman Developmental Disabilities Services Act in that letter
you asked for a waiver so that you may become "vendorized."

The Fair Political Practices Commission is charged with
administering and implementing the Political Reform Act.1/ Your
question does not pertain to the Act. (See Adams Advice Letters
I-93-174, I-93-193 and I-92-653, mailed to you earlier.) We are
therefore returning your request for advice unanswered. Your
request should be directed to the agency responsible for
implementing the Lanterman Act. You may want to contact the
Department of Developmental Services at 1600 9th Street,
Sacramento, CA 95814.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact the
Commission's Legal Division at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

JET/it
Enclosure

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000 et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660
